definition_en: A set of rules followed by a computer to solve a problem
example: The recommendation algorithm shows you content based on your history.

definition_en: Computer systems that can perform tasks that normally require human intelligence
example: Artificial intelligence is changing the way businesses operate.

definition_en: A type of AI where computers learn from data without being explicitly programmed
example: Machine learning allows the app to improve over time.

definition_en: Storing and accessing data over the internet instead of on a local computer
example: Cloud computing allows teams to work together from anywhere.

definition_en: The practice of protecting systems and networks from digital attacks
example: Companies invest heavily in cybersecurity to protect user data.

definition_en: The amount of data that can be transmitted through a network connection
example: You need high bandwidth to stream 4K video smoothly.

definition_en: To convert data into a code to prevent unauthorised access
example: All messages are encrypted end-to-end.

definition_en: The point of interaction between a user and a computer system
example: The new interface is much easier to navigate.

definition_en: The use of technology to perform tasks without human involvement
example: Automation has replaced many routine manufacturing jobs.

definition_en: Programs and operating systems used by a computer
example: They developed new software to manage customer data.

definition_en: The physical components of a computer system
example: The hardware needs upgrading to run this software.

definition_en: A computer that stores and provides data to other computers
example: The company runs its own dedicated server.

definition_en: A structured collection of data stored and accessed electronically
example: All customer records are stored in the database.

definition_en: A computer-generated simulation of a three-dimensional environment
example: Virtual reality headsets are used for gaming and training.

definition_en: A program that simulates human conversation using AI
example: The customer service chatbot answered most common questions automatically.

definition_en: The right to control how personal information is collected and used
example: New laws aim to protect users' data privacy online.

definition_en: An error or flaw in a computer program
example: The developers fixed the bug that was causing crashes.

definition_en: A new version of software with improvements or fixes
example: Install the latest update to keep your device secure.

definition_en: A digital record-keeping system that is secure and decentralised
example: Blockchain technology is used to record cryptocurrency transactions.

definition_en: The state of being connected to a network or the internet
example: Poor connectivity made video calls very difficult.

definition_en: A text input given to an AI system to generate a response
example: The quality of the AI output depends on the prompt you write.

definition_en: A tendency for AI to produce unfair or skewed results due to flawed training data
example: Researchers are working to reduce bias in AI models.
